data.title: BlackRock has seen a net inflow of 17,642 Bitcoins since February 24.
data.description: According to Lookonchain, BlackRock has been accumulating Bitcoin recently, with a net inflow of 17,642 Bitcoins, worth $1.28 billion, since February 24.

data.title: Tether's USAT Stablecoin Achieves Regulatory Milestone with Deloitte Attestation
data.description: Tether has secured an attestation report from Deloitte for its US-regulated stablecoin, USAT, marking a significant regulatory achievement. According to NS3.AI, this development highlights Tether's efforts to enhance transparency and compliance within the regulatory framework. However, the report does not extend to USDT, Tether's primary and globally dominant stablecoin, which holds $189 billion in reserves that remain unaudited by a Big Four accounting firm. USDT continues to face scrutiny regarding its transparency, complex reserve composition, and regulatory challenges.

data.title: Bitcoin's Thin Supply Zone Could Trigger Rapid Rally
data.description: Bitcoin's order book data indicates a notably thin supply zone between $72,000 and $80,000. According to NS3.AI, this scarcity of sell orders suggests minimal resistance in that range, potentially enabling a swift price advance. Traders are closely monitoring the situation for a breakout that could lead to a rapid rally toward $80,000.

data.title: UniSat will launch an upgrade to the Ordinals system to enhance infrastructure and product experience.
data.description: UniSat has announced a system upgrade to the latest version of Ordinals, covering the index layer, protocol support layer, product layer, and API layer. This upgrade, expected to be completed gradually over the next few weeks, aims to improve the standardization and on-chain data representation capabilities of the Ordinals ecosystem. At the infrastructure level, UniSat is upgrading its Ordinals index to the latest version, providing a data foundation for supporting new features while improving index stability and scalability. Upon completion, the index will support the newly introduced Traits feature in Ordinals, enabling attribute-based filtering in transactions and enhancing the expressiveness and composability of aggregate assets. The next version of UniSat Wallet will add support for importing from other major wallets; UniSat Marketplace will gradually implement several feature upgrades over the next few weeks; and UniSat Inscribe will support the new on-chain aggregate inscription, simplifying the existing inscription process. The updated UniSat API will simultaneously support the aforementioned new features, including querying new on-chain data such as Traits, minting on-chain aggregates, and enabling zero-fee transactions.

data.title: In the past hour, $113 million in liquidations occurred across the entire network, with $76.74 million in BTC liquidations.
data.description: According to Coinglass data, $113 million in positions were liquidated across the network in the past hour, including $102 million in short positions and $10.86 million in long positions. BTC liquidations reached $76.74 million and ETH liquidations reached $34.57 million.

data.title: Bitcoin(BTC) Surpasses 73,000 USDT with a 8.60% Increase in 24 Hours
data.description: On Mar 04, 2026, 15:32 PM(UTC). According to Binance Market Data, Bitcoin has crossed the 73,000 USDT benchmark and is now trading at 73,027.90625 USDT, with a narrowed 8.60% increase in 24 hours.

data.title: U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve Remains Unchanged
data.description: The U.S. Strategic Petroleum Reserve (SPR) inventory remained unchanged for the week ending February 27, according to Jin10. The inventory level stood at 0 million barrels, consistent with the previous week's figures. This stability in the SPR comes amid ongoing discussions about energy security and market dynamics. The unchanged reserve levels may reflect current government strategies or market conditions influencing oil storage decisions. The SPR is a critical component of the U.S. energy policy, serving as a buffer against potential supply disruptions.

data.title: Wintermute analyst: Capital has begun a phase of shift towards crypto assets; BTC's outperformance of US stocks may be due to fund rotation.
data.description: Wintermute analyst Jasper De Maere stated that the significant underperformance of crypto assets compared to other asset classes over the past two months may be one reason for the current relative strength divergence. He believes that digital assets differ from stocks and are not directly affected by macroeconomic narratives such as supply chains and energy costs, which becomes a relative advantage in the current market environment. Stocks and crypto assets have gradually become "alternative risk assets." Against the backdrop of uncertainty suppressing equity inflows, some capital may be rotating into digital assets. However, he also warned that this outperformance may not be sustainable. If geopolitical tensions lead to further increases in energy prices and push up inflation, weakening expectations of interest rate cuts, it could put pressure on the crypto market. In the short term, the market will remain highly volatile, awaiting a clearer macroeconomic direction.

data.title: Middle East Oil Producers Face Countdown Amid Iran Conflict
data.description: Oil-producing countries in the Middle East are facing a tense countdown due to the ongoing conflict in Iran, which has blocked a major export artery in the region. According to Jin10, if the situation persists, storage tanks will reach capacity, forcing these nations to cut production. Iraq, with limited storage capacity, has already begun significant production cuts. JPMorgan warns that other countries like Saudi Arabia and the UAE may need to follow suit within weeks. A joint military action by the U.S. and Israel has driven London Brent crude futures to a 19-month high, exceeding $85 per barrel. More severe production cuts could exacerbate the price surge and increase inflationary pressures on the global economy.

data.title: UK Considers Military Action Against Iran to Deplete Missile Stockpile
data.description: The UK is reportedly considering military action against Iran to deplete its missile stockpile, according to a Western official cited by The Times. This potential move comes amid heightened tensions in the region and ongoing concerns about Iran's missile capabilities. According to Jin10, the official indicated that the UK has not ruled out participating in strikes aimed at reducing Iran's missile arsenal. The situation remains fluid, and further developments are anticipated as diplomatic and military strategies are evaluated.

data.title: Japan's Financial Services Agency Investigates Solana-Based Memecoin
data.description: Japan's Financial Services Agency is reportedly investigating Sanae Token, a Solana-based memecoin named after Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi. According to NS3.AI, the investigation stems from concerns about the token's lack of proper exchange licensing. Launched by the NoBorder DAO, Sanae Token has experienced a significant surge in price and market capitalization. However, it remains unapproved by both regulators and the government, potentially violating Japan's Payment Services Act. This situation mirrors similar controversies in the United States involving politician-themed tokens, underscoring the regulatory challenges associated with memecoin projects linked to political figures.

data.title: U.S. Service Economy Sees Strongest Growth Since Mid-2022
data.description: The U.S. service sector experienced its most rapid expansion in February since mid-2022, driven by significant increases in orders and business activity. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting the sector's robust performance as a key indicator of economic health. This growth suggests a positive outlook for the service economy, reflecting increased demand and business confidence. The expansion marks a notable recovery, indicating resilience in the face of broader economic challenges. Analysts view this development as a sign of sustained momentum in the service industry, which plays a crucial role in the overall U.S. economy.

data.title: Poland's Central Bank Updates GDP Growth Forecasts for 2026 and 2027
data.description: Poland's central bank has revised its GDP growth forecasts for the coming years. According to Jin10, the bank now anticipates the GDP growth rate for 2026 to be between 3.1% and 4.7%, an increase from the previous forecast of 2.7% to 4.6% made in November. For 2027, the growth rate is expected to range from 2.0% to 3.8%, compared to the earlier prediction of 1.5% to 3.7%.

data.title: Poland's Central Bank Reports Recent Global Energy Price Increase
data.description: Poland's central bank has observed a recent rise in global energy prices. According to Jin10, this development comes amid ongoing fluctuations in the energy market, impacting various sectors worldwide. The increase in energy costs is a significant factor influencing economic conditions, with potential implications for inflation and monetary policy decisions. The central bank's report highlights the need for careful monitoring of energy price trends as they continue to evolve.

data.title: White House Advocates for Clarity Act to Benefit Cryptocurrency
data.description: The White House is actively promoting the passage of the Clarity Act, a move seen as beneficial for the cryptocurrency sector. BitMine Chair Tom Lee posted on X, highlighting the potential positive impact on digital currencies such as Ethereum ($ETH). The Clarity Act aims to provide clearer regulatory guidelines for cryptocurrencies, which could foster innovation and growth within the industry. This legislative push is part of a broader effort to integrate digital currencies into the mainstream financial system, ensuring they are regulated effectively while encouraging technological advancement.

data.title: Bloomberg Invest Concludes with Key Industry Leaders
data.description: Bloomberg posted on X that the Bloomberg Invest event has concluded, featuring insights from prominent industry leaders. The event included discussions with Dilhan Pillay Sandrasegara from Temasek, Yie-Hsin from State Street Global Advisors, and representatives from Apollo Global Management. These leaders shared their perspectives on current market trends and investment strategies, providing valuable insights into the financial landscape. The event highlighted the importance of adapting to changing economic conditions and the role of innovation in driving growth. Participants emphasized the need for strategic planning and collaboration to navigate the complexities of the global market.

data.title: U.S. Non-Manufacturing Prices Index Declines in February
data.description: The U.S. non-manufacturing prices index fell to 63 in February, down from the previous value of 66.6. According to Jin10, this decrease indicates a potential easing in price pressures within the non-manufacturing sector. The index, which measures price changes in the services sector, is closely watched by economists and policymakers as an indicator of inflation trends. The decline may suggest a shift in market dynamics, impacting future economic forecasts and monetary policy decisions. Analysts will be monitoring upcoming data releases to assess the broader implications for the U.S. economy.

data.title: Binance.US launches NPCs
data.description: Binance.US has announced the listing of NPC, and the NPC/USDT trading pair is now open for trading.

data.title: U.S. Non-Manufacturing New Orders Index Rises in February
data.description: The U.S. non-manufacturing new orders index increased to 58.6 in February, up from the previous value of 53.1. According to Jin10, this rise indicates a strengthening in the non-manufacturing sector, reflecting increased demand and economic activity. The index, which measures new orders in the service sector, suggests a positive outlook for the U.S. economy as businesses experience growth in new orders. This development is seen as a sign of economic resilience amid ongoing challenges in the global market.

data.title: RedStone has deployed price oracles on the Stellar network.
data.description: Following a $10 million oracle vulnerability attack, oracle service provider RedStone announced the deployment of a price oracle on the Stellar network. This deployment aims to enhance the security and functionality of the Stellar ecosystem while providing developers with a reliable source of price information. (Cointelegraph)
